keyPoints: Supervised learning uses labeled data to train a model for text classification.
Common algorithms include Naive Bayes, Support Vector Machines (SVM), and Logistic Regression.
The process involves feature extraction (like Bag-of-Words or TF-IDF), model training, and prediction on new, unlabeled text.
commonMistakes: Ignoring text preprocessing steps like stopword removal and stemming, which can significantly impact model performance.
Failing to properly handle class imbalance in the training data, leading to a biased classifier.
Using the test data during the feature extraction or model training phase, which causes data leakage and overly optimistic results.
